skill.name: specdd-author
skill.description: Creates and revises SpecDD .sdd specification files in existing SpecDD projects.
skill.summary: AI guidance for authoring SpecDD specification files with proper bootstrap resolution and write authority rules.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: All 16 static analysis findings are false positives. The 15 external_commands backtick detections are standard Markdown inline code formatting (file paths like .specdd/bootstrap.md and keywords like References), not Ruby shell execution. The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm finding on line 3 is a YAML description field with no cryptographic content. The skill's intended filesystem operations (reading .specdd/ bootstrap files and writing .sdd spec files) are legitimate and well-documented with appropriate safety constraints.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Read and Write Access
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI to read .specdd/ bootstrap files and write .sdd specification files. This is the intended purpose of the skill and is properly constrained by documented authority rules. Filesystem access is limited to spec-related files within the project.

content.user_title: Author and Revise SpecDD Specification Files
content.value_statement: Writing SpecDD specification files requires careful adherence to project conventions and authority rules. This skill guides the AI to resolve bootstrap chains, respect write authority, and create concise behavioral specs.
actual_capabilities: Resolves active bootstrap chain from project spec files before any edit
Creates new .sdd spec files within granted write authority only
Revises existing specs while respecting ownership and contract rules
Walks ancestor spec chains for proper context resolution
Identifies nearest local spec with write authority before making changes
Reports on bootstrap files used, specs changed, and unresolved decisions
limitations: Cannot adopt SpecDD in a new project without existing .specdd/bootstrap.md
Cannot modify implementation files unless explicitly requested by the user
Cannot infer write authority from filenames or symbols without explicit declarations
Requires active bootstrap chain to determine proper spec hierarchy

use_cases.title: Adding New Specs to a Feature Area
use_cases.description: When a developer needs to document new behavior or constraints for an existing feature area in a SpecDD project.
use_cases.target_user: Developer working on a SpecDD project

use_cases.title: Revising Outdated Specifications
use_cases.description: When existing spec files need updates to match current behavior, changed requirements, or new project contracts.

use_cases.title: Spec Discovery for New Team Members
use_cases.description: When a new team member needs to understand the spec chain, ownership rules, and write authority for a target area before making changes.
use_cases.target_user: New engineer joining a SpecDD project

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Spec File
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new .sdd spec file for the [feature] area. First resolve the bootstrap chain, then find the nearest governing spec that grants write authority. Create a concise behavioral spec with ownership declarations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding specification for a new feature in an existing project

prompt_templates.title: Update an Existing Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Review and update the spec file at [path]. Ensure it accurately describes current behavior, constraints, and ownership rules. Do not change authority boundaries without confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Spec Chain Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk the ancestor spec chain for [target path] starting from the root bootstrap. List which specs grant write authority and summarize their ownership rules.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Write Authority Before Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Before editing [target path], verify that write authority is explicitly granted by the nearest local spec. If authority is unclear, stop and list the unresolved questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Safe editing in an unfamiliar spec area

output_examples.input: Add a spec for the new search filtering feature.
output_examples.output: Resolved the bootstrap chain from .specdd/bootstrap.md. Found nearest governing spec at search/contract.sdd with Owns search/ authority. Created search/filtering.sdd with behavioral constraints for the search filter logic. Write authority was confirmed before any edits.

output_examples.input: Review the specs for the user authentication module.
output_examples.output: Read bootstrap chain and resolved governing specs for auth/. Found 3 spec files: auth/login.sdd with login authority, auth/session.sdd for session management, and auth/permissions.sdd for access control. All specs have valid Can modify declarations. No authority conflicts found.

best_practices: Always resolve the bootstrap chain before creating or editing any spec file
Keep specs short, local, and focused on behavioral constraints rather than implementation details
Use explicit Can modify and Owns declarations to make write authority discoverable
anti_patterns: Inferring write authority from similar filenames or nearby files instead of explicit declarations
Creating specs without first identifying the nearest governing spec and its ownership rules
Turning uncertain observations into durable contracts in project spec files

faq.question: What is SpecDD?
faq.answer: SpecDD is Specification-Driven Development. Project behavior and constraints are defined in .sdd specification files that guide development work.

faq.question: When should I use this skill instead of specdd-adopt?
faq.answer: Use this skill only for projects that already have a .specdd/bootstrap.md file. Use specdd-adopt to set up SpecDD in a new or non-SpecDD project.

faq.question: Can this skill modify implementation code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only creates and edits .sdd spec files. It will not change implementation files unless you explicitly ask for implementation work.

faq.question: What happens if write authority is unclear?
faq.answer: If write authority, ownership, or security scope is not clear, the skill stops and asks for clarification before making any changes.

faq.question: How does the skill decide which spec files to edit?
faq.answer: The skill walks the ancestor spec chain from the target area and identifies the nearest spec that grants explicit write authority through Can modify or Owns declarations.

faq.question: Does this skill require any CLI tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill operates by reading and writing spec files directly. No external tools or commands are needed.
